title: Password
description: Password is a field-specialized wrapper around the shared input foundation with built-in show or hide behavior, clear action, and native password autocomplete support.
intro: Use `ui-password` for sign-in, reset, and credential-management flows where masked entry, reveal actions, and password-manager compatibility matter. It inherits the shared field API for labels, help text, size, variant, readonly, disabled, and validation messaging while adding password-specific affordances.

featureSections.title: Basic password fields
featureSections.description: Use the standard field for sign-in and quick verification, then rely on the built-in reveal and clear actions when the value is editable.

featureSections.title: Layout, size, and variant
featureSections.description: Password fields follow the same density and label rhythm as the rest of the field family, so they can fit dense admin surfaces and roomier setup flows without custom layout work.

featureSections.title: Autocomplete and reveal behavior
featureSections.description: Use the right autocomplete token for current versus new passwords. Readonly values stay visible but intentionally suppress editing, reveal, and clear actions.

featureSections.title: Strength guidance
featureSections.description: The component does not enforce password policy by itself, but it works well with helper text, error text, and lightweight visual guidance layered beside the field.

featureSections.title: Confirmation and matching
featureSections.description: A practical pairing for account creation and recovery flows: validate minimum password rules in the first field and keep the second field focused on exact matching.

featureSections.title: Reset panel composition
featureSections.description: A stronger end-to-end example: current password, new password, confirmation, and direct actions inside one contained reset surface.

accessibility.title: Label and name
accessibility.body: Prefer a visible `label` so the purpose of the secret is explicit. Help and error text contribute through `aria-describedby`, and `ariaLabel` can be used when no visible label is appropriate.

accessibility.title: Keyboard and field actions
accessibility.body:
This is a standard text-entry field backed by a native password input. Reveal and clear actions are exposed as separate buttons only when the field is editable.

| Aspect | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| focus | native password input receives focus |
| typing | native password input behavior |
| reveal action | toggles the input type between `password` and `text` |
| clear action | appears only when the field has a value and is editable |
| readonly / disabled | actions are hidden and direct editing is blocked |

accessibility.title: Autocomplete semantics
accessibility.body: Use `autocomplete="current-password"` for sign-in or identity verification and `autocomplete="new-password"` for creation or reset flows. This improves browser autofill and password-manager behavior without changing field layout.

apiSections.title: PasswordComponent - ui-password
apiSections.lead: Standalone password field built on the shared field base. It adds reveal and clear affordances while preserving the same layout, validation, and form-control model as the other field components.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Hint shown when the value is empty.
Marks the native input as required.
Keeps the value visible but not editable.
Use password-specific tokens such as `current-password` or `new-password`.
Visual treatment of the input surface.

paragraphs: The component extends the shared `FieldComponent`, so CVA integration, focus handling, described-by wiring, and validation behavior match the rest of the field family.
Reveal and clear actions are built into the field chrome and are only rendered when the field is editable. This keeps readonly and disabled secrets from exposing unnecessary action buttons.
